title: Composition Candidates To Add
description: Tracks new compositions (frameworks, services, protocols, applications) surfaced during scans or pattern authoring but not yet catalogued in compositions-src/. The symmetric analog of pattern-todo.json for the composition layer. Distinct from compositions-todo.json, which is a pattern-centric usage-coverage worklist (which existing patterns lack a documented usage). Each entry names a candidate composition, the patterns it would implement, and the upstream evidence. Entries graduate by being authored into compositions-src/ and removed from this list.

id.description: Proposed kebab-case composition id. Must not collide with any existing composition id in compositions-src/.

kind_hint.description: Whether this candidate is a recipe (a reusable pattern arrangement) or a framework/product/service/protocol.

category_hint.description: Suggested composition category (e.g. orchestration-framework, voice-conversational, agent-sdk). Optional — authoring may re-classify.

summary.description: One or two sentences naming what the composition is and what it does.

suggested_members.description: Catalog patterns this composition is expected to implement, with optional role and note. These become members[] when authored.

pattern.description: Existing or proposed pattern id this composition would implement.

raised_by.description: What surfaced this candidate, and the upstream evidence. The source is the pattern id whose known_uses/references named it, or 'global-scan' when the wider literature surfaced it.

source.description: Pattern id that referenced this composition, or 'global-scan' for literature-surfaced candidates.

status.description: Lifecycle: proposed = on the list; drafting = entry being written in compositions-src/; authored = lives in compositions-src/ now (this candidate can be deleted); rejected = considered and not pursued, with a reason.

needs_verification.description: True when the candidate was surfaced from a secondary mention and its own primary source has not yet been content-verified. Must be resolved to false (with a content-verified primary source) before authoring.
